# UC-22.7.29 · DNS Resolver Error Rate SLO for Internal Resolvers

## Description

Tracks DNS infrastructure error rates as resilience signal for critical name resolution dependency.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST CSF compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes dns and sourcetypes dns:query. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Set SLO thresholds; (2) correlate with DDoS; (3) include in DR tabletop; (4) document failover. Indexes required: `index=dns`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=dns:query`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: BIND or CoreDNS logs.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `dns:query`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Set SLO thresholds; (2) correlate with DDoS; (3) include in DR tabletop; (4) document failover.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=dns sourcetype=dns:query earliest=-24h
| eval fail=if(match(_raw,"(?i)SERVFAIL|REFUSED"),1,0)
| timechart span=1h sum(fail) as failures
```

Understanding this SPL

**DNS Resolver Error Rate SLO for Internal Resolvers (PR.IR-01)** — Tracks DNS infrastructure error rates as resilience signal for critical name resolution dependency.

Documented **Data sources**: `dns:query`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): BIND or CoreDNS logs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: dns; **sourcetype**: dns:query. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=dns, sourcetype=dns:query, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **fail** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1h** buckets — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart, Single value

## SPL

```spl
index=dns sourcetype=dns:query earliest=-24h
| eval fail=if(match(_raw,"(?i)SERVFAIL|REFUSED"),1,0)
| timechart span=1h sum(fail) as failures
```

## Visualization

Time chart, Single value. Key fields: `fail`.
